title: phanera sulphurea
intro: Phanera sulphurea, also known as the "Golden Rain Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phanera sulphurea include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. The plant is traditionally prepared into herbal remedies, including a decoction made by boiling the bark and leaves in water, which is used to treat various ailments.
